race.name: Granfondo Antalya
race.display_name: UCI Gran Fondo World Series Antalya

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 71.

course_description.character: Start and finish sit outside The Land of Legends resort in the Kadriye/Belek district east of Antalya. The route stays in the Turkish Riviera's low, rolling hinterland rather than climbing into the Taurus Mountains — the 2025 long course (92.6 km/512 m) ran from Prof. Dr. Erdal İnönü Kent Parkı to Düden Waterfall through the Lara–Aksu/EXPO corridor and Kurşunlu-area backroads, mixing fast flat/rolling tempo sections with a handful of short, sharper rises around Aksu and Kurşunlu. The course has changed materially edition to edition — 98 km/517 m in 2024, 92.6 km/512 m in 2025, a reported 105 km/658 m for 2026 — so treat any single year's vitals as edition-specific, not fixed.
course_description.signature_challenge: No single decisive climb — the course rewards sustained bunch speed across repeated shallow rollers rather than a marquee mountain pass; the closest thing to a defining test is the string of short Kurşunlu-area rises (documented 2025 examples run roughly 1.4-4.0 km at 2.8-6.0%).
suffering_zones: EXPO-road tempo sections — long, flat-to-gently-rolling stretches (documented 2025: ~11 km/+13 m then ~10 km/+9 m) where positioning in the bunch matters more than power
Kurşunlu/Güloluk climb cluster — the route's sharpest rises, documented in 2025 at roughly 1.4-4.0 km, 2.8-6.0% average
The time trial — a short (2025: 22.8 km), nearly flat individual effort held as a separate discipline alongside the gran fondo and short course

biased_opinion.verdict: A Fast, Flat UCI Qualifier — Just Don't Trust the Vitals
biased_opinion.summary: UCI Gran Fondo World Series Antalya (formerly branded Nirvana Granfondo Antalya, after title sponsor Nirvana Hotels) is a real, currently active UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier held every November in the Kadriye/Belek resort corridor east of Antalya. It delivers what it promises: closed-road racing, genuine UCI World Championships qualification for the top 25% of finishers by age group, and warm late-autumn weather on the Turkish Riviera. What it does not deliver is a stable course — published distance and elevation have shifted every year on record (98 km/517 m in 2024, 92.6 km/512 m in 2025, a reported 105 km/658 m for 2026), and even within a single season the organizer's own pages have disagreed with the federation calendar and the UCI listing on both distance and dates. This is a fast, rolling course for rouleurs, not a mountain test — 658 m of climbing across 105 km is thin for a 'gran fondo' billing, and the organizer publishes no detailed climb map or gradient breakdown for the current route.
strengths: Genuine UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier — top 25% of age-group finishers advance to the UCI Gran Fondo World Championships
Fully closed, traffic-free roads with motorcycle security and first aid on course
Resort-zone start/finish next to Antalya's airport and extensive hotel infrastructure makes international travel unusually convenient
Fast, rolling course suits sprinters and rouleurs who don't want a mountain fondo
weaknesses: Course length and climbing have changed every documented year — 98 km/517 m (2024), 92.6 km/512 m (2025), a reported 105 km/658 m (2026) — so any vitals quoted here are edition-specific, not a fixed course
Very little climbing for a race marketed as a 'gran fondo' — under 700 m across the long course, concentrated in a handful of short rises rather than any real mountain test
Organizer, federation calendar, and UCI listing have published conflicting dates and distances within the same season; no detailed current-year route map or gradient data is publicly available
This entry was previously duplicated under a legacy 'Nirvana Granfondo Antalya' listing in this database — the two profiles describe the same race under different sponsor-era names
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Take this one for the UCI qualification and the fast, flat racing on a well-supported course near Antalya's airport — not for mountain terrain, and not for confidence in any single published distance figure until the current year's official route page is live.

final_verdict.one_liner: Warm late-season UCI qualifier on Antalya's flats near a major resort corridor — but check the current year's course, it moves every edition.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a genuine UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier on fast, flat-to-rolling roads with warm November weather and convenient resort/airport logistics. Skip it if you want real Turkish mountain climbing — this course stays in the coastal hinterland, not the Taurus range.
final_verdict.alternatives: For genuine climbing in Turkey, look at Alanya's Taurus-facing routes or Cappadocia-region gran fondos. For a similarly fast, resort-adjacent UCI qualifier with more stable course data, compare against other UCI Gran Fondo World Series stops.

eligibility.notes: UCI's own 2026 competition listing confirms 6-8 November 2026 (road races 8 November) as UCI Gran Fondo World Series Antalya; distance/elevation updated to the reported 2026 long-course figures (105 km/658 m), up from 92.6 km/512 m in 2025 and 98 km/517 m in 2024 — this course changes materially every edition. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research as part of the Roadie Labs editorial wave 3.; last edition evidenced: 2025 (2026 is scheduled, not yet run as of this research)

catalog_flags.status_note: Dedupe sweep 2026-07-24: this profile absorbs the duplicate entry previously filed separately as 'Gran Fondo Nirvana Antalya' (race-data/gran-fondo-nirvana-antalya.json), which described the same race under its former Nirvana Hotels sponsor-era name. That entry has been flagged duplicate_of this profile and left as a stub.
